item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Way too vague. I can't even add any more tags beyond spacetime and location, solely for the assumption that people get lost because of it.

E: rereading this, I feel like there's a large amount of loose ends that could lead to different interpretations. I don't dislike ambiguity, but there isn't enough to form a solid perspective, like as TL333s says.

Also, this needs to be titled on the Series list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. Although the number escapes me, this reminded me of a semi-recent one involving exploration logs of hikers in a national park who encounter weird deer to the point where I thought it might be a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs more to it. Given the honestly uninspiring description, this is the kind of article that lives and dies by its exploration logs. You're off to a good start with the first two, but it's not enough. There's no story, no logic thread, nothing beyond "it's another spooky forest where things go wrong". I wish I could remember what number the recent one was with the pipes and dirt and this probably isn't helping, but you need something more along the lines of that in structure for this to both work and stand out from similar articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Why would senior staff/valuable personnel be permitted to enter a highly dangerous anomaly?

2. Read into the subtlety. As dankaar said, all personnel will be treated lost should they enter SCP-3809. No exceptions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A conclusive description of these phenomena is impossible, as all personnel who enter SCP-3809 are invariably lost with no hope of recovery.

That's why no recovery attempts are made. The subtext is that attempting to recover personnel is is futile, and all you'd get are search teams that are never recovered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personnel requesting assistance from within SCP-3809, regardless of their affiliation, should be ignored by all nearby staff. No recovery attempts should be made, nor will they be authorized by site security.

Honestly, this doesn't seem right. what if senior ataff or valuable personnel were in there? we wouldnt just let them die. Other than this bit, awesome article.

item.title: Nice Article
item.content:encoded: I have to admit you have made a fine article, but the containment procedures seem a tiny bit too much, you've earned my vote.
